title: Fail-closed custom-op evidence gate with per-unit NPU runtime rows
problem_description: The repair trajectory included a custom-op final evidence gate failure where project_e2e_passed, report_parity_passed, and non-empty per-unit rows were not closed. This matters because custom-op migrations can otherwise falsely pass with summary reports, missing source inventory linkage, zero-call evidence, or CPU/CUDA fallback paths.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Build a source-driven fine-grained operator inventory and a matching migration manifest. 2. Generate custom_op_final_gate.json from same-run runtime coverage and performance reports, not from static summaries alone. 3. Include project_e2e_passed, report_parity_passed, source_inventory, performance_report, and one row per required unit. 4. For every row, include artifact, adapter, parity, integration/runtime coverage, performance, and no-fallback evidence. 5. Return nonzero unless every required unit executed on NPU, has parity and performance evidence, and reports no CPU/CUDA routing.

involved_code_files.role: Final gate generator — emits source inventory, performance report, per-unit rows, no-fallback flags, and fail-closed status

reasoning: This is reusable as a prompt/checklist improvement for future custom-op migrations: require per-unit same-run NPU execution evidence and reject report-only or fallback success. It is verified in this run by final exit_code=0, FULL_PASS, 9 closed rows, and no CPU/CUDA routed units. It is ranked medium rather than high because it is a validation/process lesson rather than the operator implementation technique itself.
